question: How do I set up the Zorali backend for local development?

documents.text: To run the Zorali backend locally, install the requirements with pip install -r backend/requirements.txt. Then start the server with PYTHONPATH=backend uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000. The backend will be available at http://localhost:8000.
Before starting, copy .env.example to .env and set your SECRET_KEY. The POSTGRES_HOST should point to your local PostgreSQL instance. Redis must be running at the URL specified in REDIS_URL.
The artifact versioning system stores every edit as an immutable version. Use POST /api/artifacts to create, and PUT /api/artifacts/{id} to add a new version.
WebSocket streaming is available at /ws/chat/{session_id}. Send JSON with role and message fields. Tokens stream back as type=token events.
File uploads are limited to 5 MB. Supported types include .txt, .md, .json, .csv, .py, .js, .ts, .html, .css, .pdf.

question: What retrieval algorithm does Zorali use for file search?

documents.text: Zorali uses a two-stage hybrid retrieval pipeline. Stage 1 combines BM25 and TF-IDF rankings via Reciprocal Rank Fusion. Stage 2 applies a LexicalFeatureReranker that scores IDF-weighted term coverage, exact phrase matches, proximity, and bigram overlap.

Optional dense semantic search is available when RAG_EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED=true. Dense embeddings are generated using Ollama with the nomic-embed-text model. The asymmetric retrieval uses search_document prefix for indexing and search_query prefix for queries.
Projects can be created via POST /api/project with a name and optional description. Each project gets a UUID that scopes all files, artifacts, and chat history.
The learning loop records traces of user interactions locally. Traces include latency, token count, and optional user ratings. LEARNING_LOOP_ENABLED controls whether tracing is active.
Docker Compose deployment requires running docker compose up --build, then pulling the Ollama model with docker compose exec ollama ollama pull llama3.2:1b.

question: How does the rate limiting system work in Zorali?

documents.text: Zorali implements a token-bucket rate limiter. RATE_LIMIT_CAPACITY controls the maximum burst size in tokens. RATE_LIMIT_REFILL sets the token replenishment rate per second. Each client IP is tracked independently.
Artifact content is stored as versioned snapshots. GET /api/artifacts/{id} returns all versions. The latest version is always at versions[-1].
The frontend is built with React and Vite. Run npm ci then npm run dev from the frontend directory to start the dev server on port 5173.
PDF extraction currently uses a latin-1 decode approach to find text inside parentheses. For production use, install pypdf for proper page-aware extraction.
The memory pool is inspired by vLLM PagedAttention. MEMORY_POOL_BLOCKS and MEMORY_POOL_BLOCK_SIZE control the pool size. This affects how many inference requests can run concurrently.

question: What configuration variables control the hybrid search behavior?

documents.text: RAG_RERANK_ENABLED toggles the lexical feature reranker. RAG_CANDIDATE_POOL sets how many candidates enter the reranking stage. RAG_RRF_K is the constant in the Reciprocal Rank Fusion formula. RAG_RERANK_WEIGHT controls the multiplicative boost applied by lexical features.

RAG_RANKER_GUARANTEE ensures the top N results from each individual ranker always enter the candidate pool even if their RRF score is low. RAG_DENSE_RRF_WEIGHT gives the dense ranker extra voting weight compared to each lexical ranker.
Chat messages are persisted per project and session. The session_id scopes the conversation context window. All messages for a project can be retrieved via the repository.
The task queue system is inspired by Higgsfield experiment queues. TASK_QUEUE_MAX_CONCURRENT limits parallel task execution. TASK_QUEUE_COST_BUDGET_USD caps spending per run.
Cloud fallback activates when the local Ollama model is unavailable. Set CLOUD_API_KEY in .env to enable it. CLOUD_API_BASE must point to an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

question: How is contextual embedding done at upload time?

documents.text: When RAG_CONTEXTUAL_ENABLED is true, each chunk is prefixed with a document-level context header before embedding. The header is formed from the filename and top document keywords: 'filename.txt :: keyword1 keyword2 ...'. This gives each chunk global document context during retrieval.
Security: uploads are validated for size (max 5 MB), extension allowlist, and filename safety. Hidden files starting with a dot are blocked. Path traversal in project_id is rejected.
The checkpoint system persists model state to disk. CHECKPOINT_ENABLED controls whether checkpointing runs. Checkpoints follow the TensorFlow SavedModel pattern.
Skills are modular capabilities loaded at startup. SKILLS_AUTOLOAD controls automatic discovery. Each skill is a Python module exposing a skill_name and execute function.
The orchestrator routes messages to different agent modes including deep research, chat, and task execution. The agent plan is generated before the LLM call and injected as a system message.

question: What endpoints are available for managing files?

documents.text: POST /api/files/upload accepts a multipart file upload with a project_id query parameter. It returns the file record with chunk count and indexing_status. The endpoint returns HTTP 202 immediately while background embedding proceeds.

GET /api/files/list returns all files for a project. GET /api/files/search runs hybrid retrieval against file chunks. DELETE /api/files/{file_id} removes the file record and deletes the bytes from disk. GET /api/files/{file_id}/status returns the current indexing_status.
JWT tokens are issued on login with a 30-minute access token lifetime and 30-day refresh token. JWT_ACCESS_MINUTES and JWT_REFRESH_DAYS control these durations.
The inference cost budget tracks API spending. INFERENCE_COST_BUDGET_USD is the per-session limit. Cost-aware routing prefers local models when the budget is exhausted.
Memories are stored per user and project. save_memory records a text snippet. search_memories does token-overlap scoring to find relevant past memories.

question: How does BM25 ranking work in the search engine?

documents.text: BM25 ranks documents using term frequency saturation and document length normalization. The score for a term t in document d is: IDF(t) * (tf * (k1+1)) / (tf + k1 * (1 - b + b * dl/avgdl)). Zorali uses k1=1.5 and b=0.75.
The batch processor allows multiple LLM requests to be processed concurrently. BATCH_MAX_CONCURRENT controls the maximum simultaneous requests. This is inspired by vLLM continuous batching.
Projects are isolated namespaces. All files, artifacts, chats, and memories belong to a project. The project UUID is validated on every operation.
The sequential RAG chain is used for structured answer synthesis. It takes retrieved chunks and generates a grounded response with inline citations.
For web search, set WEB_SEARCH_ENABLED=true. The search provider must be configured separately. Results are injected into the agent plan before the LLM call.

question: What happens when the Ollama service is unavailable?

documents.text: When Ollama is unavailable, Zorali falls back to the configured cloud provider. The CLOUD_API_KEY must be set for this to work. CLOUD_API_BASE defaults to the OpenAI API endpoint. CLOUD_MODEL specifies the fallback model to use.
The JSON persistence layer stores all data in store.json. The JsonStore class uses a threading lock to prevent concurrent write corruption. The entire file is read and rewritten on each mutation.
Chunk text splitting uses a window of 700 characters with 100 character overlap. This ensures adjacent chunks share context at their boundaries.
The LexicalFeatureReranker applies a multiplicative boost to the RRF-fused score: final = fused_norm * (1 + rerank_weight * lex_score). This means semantic-only matches retain their full fused score.
The health endpoint GET /api/health returns service status. GET /api/ollama/health checks if the local Ollama service responds. GET /api/providers/status shows which providers are active.

question: How are artifacts versioned and stored?

documents.text: Artifacts are immutably versioned. Each update appends a new version with a timestamp rather than overwriting. The artifact record contains an id, project_id, name, and a versions array. Each version has version number, content, and created_at.

TF-IDF scores documents by how often a term appears relative to how common it is across the corpus. Rare terms get higher IDF weight. The score is the product of term frequency and inverse document frequency.
The PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS setting controls how long to wait for a provider response. Default is 60 seconds. Requests that exceed this are treated as provider failures.
The streaming chat uses FastAPI WebSocket. Tokens arrive as individual JSON frames with type=token. The final frame has type=done and includes citations.
LEARNING_MIN_IMPROVEMENT_PCT sets the minimum quality improvement required before the learning loop updates the model preference. Default is 2.0 percent.

question: How does Reciprocal Rank Fusion combine BM25 and TF-IDF rankings?

documents.text: Reciprocal Rank Fusion assigns each document a score of 1/(k + rank) for each ranker, where k is a smoothing constant (default 60). Scores across rankers are summed. Documents appearing in multiple rankings receive cumulative score boosts.

In weighted RRF, each ranker has an associated weight multiplier. Dense rankers receive RAG_DENSE_RRF_WEIGHT (default 3.0) while each lexical ranker (BM25, TF-IDF) receives weight 1.0. This compensates for dense having a single ranker vs two lexical rankers.
The ZORALI_DATA_DIR environment variable overrides the default data directory. If not set, the system tries /data first (for Docker), then falls back to a local data/ directory.
File text extraction supports .txt, .md, .json, .csv, .py, .js, .ts, .html, .css, .jsx, .tsx, .toml, .yaml, .yml, .xml, .sh, and .pdf extensions.
The project scanner in app.reality.project_scanner reads the directory structure and file statistics. It is used by the /status task command to report project health.
